events.text: Patient is a 50-year-old Hispanic female with a 20 pack-year smoking history (current smoker), ECOG performance status 0, BMI 25.9.

At age 50, patient was diagnosed with non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma (histology 8140), primary site left upper lobe (C34.1), clinical stage IVB, T3N2M1c, with bilateral adrenal and hepatic metastases.

At age 50, patient presented with persistent left shoulder pain and new-onset back pain. Imaging obtained for musculoskeletal evaluation incidentally revealed a lung mass and widespread metastatic disease. She is the youngest patient in this cohort and has a relatively limited smoking history.

At age 50, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast showed a 4.6 x 3.9 cm mass in the left upper lobe extending to the left hilum with ipsilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy. Bilateral adrenal masses (right 3.2 cm, left 2.7 cm). Multiple hepatic lesions in both lobes, largest 2.9 cm in segment VIII. Suspicious sclerotic focus in L3 vertebral body.

At age 50, PET/CT demonstrated FDG-avid left upper lobe mass (SUVmax 14.1), left hilar and mediastinal lymphadenopathy (SUVmax 9.8), bilateral adrenal masses (right SUVmax 10.4, left SUVmax 8.7), hepatic lesions (SUVmax 7.5), and L3 vertebral body (SUVmax 6.2).
At age 50, MRI brain with and without contrast showed no evidence of intracranial metastatic disease.

At age 50, bronchoscopic biopsy of left upper lobe mass with EBUS-guided sampling of station 7 and 4L lymph nodes. Left upper lobe: poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma with signet ring cell features. Station 7 and 4L: positive for metastatic adenocarcinoma. Immunohistochemistry: TTF-1 positive, Napsin-A weakly positive, CK7 positive, CK20 negative, CDX2 negative. PD-L1 TPS 10% (22C3 assay).

At age 50, next-generation sequencing (FoundationOne CDx) of tumor specimen: EGFR wild-type, ALK rearrangement not detected (confirmed by FISH), ROS1 rearrangement not detected, BRAF V600E not detected, KRAS G12C detected, TP53 R248W detected, MYC amplification detected, RB1 loss detected. No actionable driver mutations. Tumor mutational burden 7.8 mut/Mb. Microsatellite stable.

At age 50, multidisciplinary tumor board: stage IVB adenocarcinoma with extensive metastatic disease. No targetable driver mutations. PD-L1 TPS 10%. Recommended first-line carboplatin/pemetrexed/pembrolizumab. Discussed prognosis and goals of care. Patient wishes aggressive treatment.
At age 50, initiated carboplatin AUC 5, pemetrexed 500 mg/m2, and pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks. Also started zoledronic acid 4 mg IV every 4 weeks for osseous metastasis.
At age 50, after cycle 1, patient developed grade 2 nausea despite prophylactic antiemetics. Grade 1 fatigue. Antiemetic regimen augmented with olanzapine.
At age 50, after cycle 2, patient developed grade 3 neutropenia (ANC 0.8 x 10^9/L). Received pegfilgrastim. Carboplatin dose reduced to AUC 4 for subsequent cycles.

At age 50, restaging CT after 4 cycles showed partial response. Left upper lobe mass decreased to 3.0 x 2.6 cm. Adrenal masses decreased bilaterally. Hepatic lesions decreased. Encouraging systemic response.
At age 50, completed 4 cycles of induction. Transitioned to pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 plus pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks maintenance.
At age 51, during maintenance cycle 3, patient developed grade 1 skin rash and grade 1 pruritus. Managed with cetirizine and moisturizers.

At age 51, restaging CT after 4 cycles of maintenance showed continued partial response. Left upper lobe mass 2.4 x 2.0 cm. Adrenal and hepatic metastases continued to shrink. L3 sclerotic focus stable.
At age 51, restaging CT after 8 cycles of maintenance showed stable partial response. No new lesions.

At age 51, excellent and durable response to pemetrexed/pembrolizumab maintenance. Patient tolering treatment well with only mild adverse effects. ECOG PS remains 0. Returned to work part-time. Will continue maintenance therapy with imaging every 9 weeks.
At age 51, after maintenance cycle 10, patient developed grade 1 hypothyroidism (TSH 9.2 mIU/L, free T4 low-normal). Started levothyroxine 25 mcg daily.

At age 51, restaging CT after 12 cycles of maintenance showed near-complete response. Left upper lobe mass now 1.2 cm linear opacity, likely fibrosis. Adrenal masses resolved. Hepatic lesions resolved. Remarkable treatment response.

At age 51, near-complete response maintained on pemetrexed/pembrolizumab. Discussed continuing maintenance versus treatment holiday. Given initial heavy disease burden, plan to continue maintenance for now with ongoing surveillance. Patient in good spirits and tolerating therapy well.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

At age 50, patient presented with persistent left shoulder pain and new-onset back pain. Imaging obtained for musculoskeletal evaluation incidentally revealed a lung mass and widespread metastatic disease. She is the youngest patient in this cohort and has a relatively limited smoking history.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Stage IIIA-IV non-small cell lung cancer without actionable driver mutations (EGFR wild-type, ALK negative, ROS1 negative). Treated with carboplatin/pemetrexed plus pembrolizumab immunotherapy. Mix of adenocarcinoma and squamous cell carcinoma. Some with high PD-L1 expression receiving pembrolizumab monotherapy.
